title: Opportunities and Challenges of Brazil-China Trade under the ...
description: Authors | Andy See, José Moscati In April 2025, US President Trump issued Executive Orders 14257 and 14259, announcing the imposition of a 10% reciprocal
extracted:
### Key Statistics on China-Brazil Trade

- **US-Brazil Trade (2024)**: US trade surplus with Brazil ~$7 billion; total value of goods and services traded $28.6 billion.
- **China-Brazil Bilateral Trade**:
  - China is Brazil’s largest trading partner; Brazil is China’s ninth largest.
  - First Latin American country whose trade volume with China exceeds USD 100 billion.
  - China invested >USD 70 billion in Brazil since overtaking US as top partner in 2009.
  - Brazil’s cumulative investment in China >USD 100 billion since 2018.
  - Average annual growth rate: 10% over past decade.
  - 2024 (first 12 months): RMB 1.34 trillion (USD 188.17 billion), up 4.6% YoY.
  - Brazil trade surplus with China: RMB 313.02 billion (USD 44,018.5 million).
- **Brazil Exports to China (2024)**: Total imports from Brazil RMB 825.84 billion (USD 116.09 billion), down 4.4% YoY.
- **China Exports to Brazil (2024)**: RMB 512.82 billion (USD 72.08 billion), up 23.3% YoY.
- **Soybeans (Brazil to China, Jan-Mar 2025)**: Brazil exports $8.7 billion total (22.18 million tonnes); 16.99 million tonnes to China (76.6% of total).
- **Oil (Brazil to China)**: 44% of Brazil’s oil exports in 2024; ~40% of quarterly oil exports Jan-Mar 2025; value of crude oil exports to China increased nearly fivefold over past decade.
- **Copper (Brazil to China, Jan-Mar 2025)**: $331 million, +180% YoY; China accounts for 35% of Brazil’s total copper exports.
- **Fertilizers (China to Brazil)**: Imports of phosphorus-, nitrogen-based fertilizers surged 625% to 265,000 tons (quarterly record).
- **New Energy Vehicles (NEVs, China to Brazil)**: First half 2024, Chinese NEVs = 91% of all NEVs imported (9 out of 10 sold).
- **Solar Panels (China to Brazil, Jan-Mar 2025)**: Imports +13% (new record).
- **Brazil Energy**: 49.1% from renewable sources.
- **Global Peace Index (GPI)**: Brazil ranks 132/163.
- **Corruption Perception Index (CPI)**: Brazil ranks 104/180.
- **Panama Canal Dependency**: 90% of China-Brazil soybean shipments.

### US Tariff Policies Impacting Trade (2025)
- **Executive Orders 14257 and 14259** (issued April 2025 by President Trump): 10% reciprocal tariff on several countries including Brazil (effective 5 April); additional 10% on certain countries from 9 April (China tariffs reached 125%).
- **Joint Statement on U.S.-China Economic and Trade Meeting in Geneva (12 May 2025)**: US to remove tariffs under EOs 14257/14259 by 14 May 2025; China tariffs temporarily adjusted to 10%.
- **Explanatory Note [1]**: Certain countries/regions may re-impose tariffs >10% after 90-day moratorium.
- Brazil subject to consistent 10% tariff (less affected than others).

### Brazil Tariff Policies Toward China/Imports
- Changes based on national interest (employment, inflation, lobbies).
- Recent: Increased tariffs, anti-dumping duties, automobile import taxes; reduction/elimination of tax exemptions for small parcels.
- **Automobile Import Tax**: Resumed 10% in Jan 2023; plans to increase to 35% by mid-2026 (Decree Nº 12.435, 15 April 2025; Gecex-Camex decision on electric cars); applies to all imports equally.
- **Textiles (Oct 2024)**: New tax policy; canceled favorable policies; imposed tariffs and anti-dumping duties on many Chinese goods.
- **Result (Dec 2024)**: >70,000 unsold Chinese electric vehicles piled up at Brazilian ports.

### Bilateral Agreements and Milestones
- **Diplomatic Milestones**:
  - 1974: Diplomatic relations established.
  - 1993: Strategic partnership.
  - 2012: Comprehensive strategic partnership.
  - 2024: Elevated to “China-Brazil Community of Shared Destiny for Building a More Just World and a More Sustainable Planet”.
- **March 2023**: Agreed to eliminate USD as intermediary; settle trade in local currencies.
- **Signed Agreements/Memorandums** (various sectors): Agriculture and livestock, agricultural products and technology, mineral energy, bio-economy, ecological transformation, green development, digital economy, artificial intelligence, photovoltaic industry.
- **Recent Discussions**:
  - 11 April 2025: Brazil VP/Minister Alckmin video call with China Minister Wang Wentao on strengthening cooperation re: US tariffs.
  - 17 April 2025: China Vice Minister Zhang Zhili led delegation to BRICS Working Group in Brazil (soybeans, beef exports; address US tariff gap); Brazil Agriculture Minister Carlos Favero: Brazil alternative beef supplier post-400 US slaughterhouses ban.
  - Early April 2025: China importers bought 40 freighters (2.4 million tonnes soybeans from Brazil; ~1/3 of China’s monthly imports; shipments May-July); 17 April: Guangzhou received ship with 38,000 tonnes Brazilian soybeans.
  - July 2025: 17th BRICS Summit in Rio de Janeiro (expected trade discussions post-US tariff suspension).
- **Belt and Road Integration**: Memoranda in economy, trade, finance, science/technology, infrastructure, environmental protection.

- ComexVis (Brazilian foreign trade data).
- Brazilian government data (oil, copper exports).
- Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China (relations overview).
